products.title: Ab Astris Texas Hill Country Estate Grown Tannat 2020
products.body_html:
This 100% Tannat grown in Ab Astris' Estate Vineyard in the Texas Hill Country spent 32 months in American Oak. Opaque magenta in color, with a bouquet of dried black fruits, sage, dark chocolate, olive tapenade and baking spices on the nose. The palate offers blueberry, blackberry, smokey black pepper and black fruit with powerful tannins, nice acidity and a slight licorice finish.

Ab Astris is a family-owned and operated winery nestled in the heart of the Texas Hill Country, crafting exceptional wines sourced from Texas-grown fruit. All of their wines are 100% Texas grown and made at their on-site production facility. They grow 12 acres of estate fruit at Ab Astris that the winemakers and owner tend to year round, and they source other fruit from reputable growers in the Hill Country and High Plains. Their winemakers strive to let the fruit tell the story of the terroir in which it came from, using minimal oak and keeping to a philosophy of aging a minimum of two years in barrel.

Ab Astris focuses on sustainable and organic-leaning farming practices, and works closely with experts on a "Roadmap to Sustainability in Texas Wine" project. They aim for environmental, social and economic sustainability in their vineyard, which includes planting cover crops for biodiversity and minimizing synthetic herbicides and pesticides.

This is the first vintage of Tannat released from the winery's estate vineyard. It recently took home gold at the San Francisco Chronicle International Wine Competition.

products.title: Agricola Caprera "Le Vasche" Cerasuolo D'Abruzzo DOC 2024
products.body_html:
This rosé of Montepulciano has a floral, earthy, umami-like nose, with aromas of rose water, citrus blossom, sour cherry and topsoil. The palate has deeper, more mature fruit flavors of cherry and strawberry, but also a balancing undertone of salty stone that sets up a finish of blood orange zest. Complex and funky. Lively acidity. Overall great wine with a glowing energy.

The name “Cerasuolo” comes from the term “cerasa,” which means cherry -- the small and delicate fruit that this wine evokes with its color and sensory notes. Cerasuolo d’Abruzzo is a unique wine that can hardly be classified within the usual wine typologies. It is rather a category in and of itself. It is a rosé wine made from Montepulciano grapes, making it a wine of contrasts by nature. Between a red – from which it takes structure and complexity – and a rosé – from which it keeps the freshness, fineness and enjoyability.

100% Montepulciano d'Abruzzo, hand-picked,  de-stemmed and pressed whole. The must ferments spontaneously and is aged in steel tanks. After bottling, it ages in the bottle for a few months before release.

Supported and inspired by scions of Italian natural wine such as Emidio Pepe and Francesco Guccione, Luca Paolo Virgilio has built a farm in Pietranico from which to explore the meaning of nature in this wild zone of Italy, where the mountains and sea seem almost to collide. Over 22 windswept hectares, dominated by forests, Luca Paolo farms just a few hectares of vines alongside olive trees, grains and beehives.

This is just a decade-old winery, but the farm is in the heart of a centuries-old region for winemaking: Pietranico, Abruzzo. Here, at 400 meters of elevation, stone crushing vats -- le vasche -- of untold age dot the agricultural landscape. This is an area almost prehistorically known for wine growing. Soils are limestone and bluish clay marls. The magic of Abruzzo is encapsulated in Caprera’s Cerasuolo: pure but pulsating, deft yet never timid.

Organic farming and low-intervention winemaking from the start, with low sulfur, spontaneous fermentations, and no oak for aging. Just a few thousand bottles made each year, these are beautifully pure, savory wines from Abruzzo -- clean, but intensely proud of their place and personality.

products.title: Albet i Noya "Petit Albet" Brut Reserva Cava 2023
products.body_html:
A Clàssic Penedès all-rounder with the traditional blend of the Penedès: Macabeo, Xarel-lo and Parellada. This certified organic Cava shows citrus and honeydew notes, with a dry finish full of mineral and sage. Aromas of herbs and fresh flowers combined with a background of perfectly ripe white fruit. In the mouth, it is refreshing but at the same time has the complexity of a sparkling wine aged for 15 months.

While Albet i Noya was established in 1978, its roots in organic craftsmanship date back to the turn of the twentieth century, when the family's expertise was already well established. Even though the phylloxera epidemic was at its height when their great-great-grandfather Joan Albet i Rovirosa arrived to work in the vineyards in Sant Pau d'Ordal, he stayed there. They are one of the few wineries left that labels their bottles with "Classic Penedès" rather than "Cava," a distinction that indicates the wine was made entirely from grapes cultivated, harvested, and vinified on the estate and matured for at least 15 months before distribution.
